title: oerstedella schumanniana
intro: Oerstedella schumanniana, also known as the Schumann's jewel orchid, is a species of orchid native to New Guinea. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to be used as an antimicrobial agent and to treat various infections. The health benefits of Oerstedella schumanniana include its potential to reduce inflammation and to have antioxidant properties. Traditional herbal preparations involve extracting the plant's roots and leaves to create remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
